orientation: Practise bounds and error intervals using the same structure from the learn lesson.

worked_example.question: A mass is 3.2 kg correct to nearest 0.1 kg. Write the interval.

questions.question: [Bounds and Error Intervals] Complete the straightforward calculation for this subtopic.
questions.hint: Follow the lesson method step by step.

steps: Identify the relevant rule from the lesson.
Apply the rule carefully to each part of the question.

questions.question: [Bounds and Error Intervals] Complete the second straightforward calculation.
questions.hint: Follow the lesson method step by step.

steps: Identify the relevant rule from the lesson.
Apply the rule carefully to each part of the question.

questions.question: [Bounds and Error Intervals] Apply the method in a two-step calculation.
questions.hint: Follow the lesson method step by step.

steps: Identify the relevant rule from the lesson.
Apply the rule carefully to each part of the question.

questions.question: [Bounds and Error Intervals] Apply the method with extra structure.
questions.hint: Follow the lesson method step by step.

steps: Identify the relevant rule from the lesson.
Apply the rule carefully to each part of the question.

questions.question: [Bounds and Error Intervals] Solve the extended GCSE-style question.
questions.hint: Follow the lesson method step by step.

steps: Identify the relevant rule from the lesson.
Apply the rule carefully to each part of the question.

common_mistakes.mistake: Skipping method steps and jumping to an answer.
common_mistakes.correction: Write each operation line by line so the method is visible.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using the wrong rule for the operation shown.
common_mistakes.correction: Identify the operation type first, then apply the matching rule.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not checking if the result is sensible.
common_mistakes.correction: Estimate or reverse-check to confirm the final answer.

summary: Apply it in clear, ordered steps.
Check the final answer before finishing.
